DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-09-2018 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Kristen A. Pico, Esq. of Hassard Bonnington LLP, T:415.288.9800, for defendant/moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling:

DEFENDANT SUTTER BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC'S DEMURRER to the second cause of action in the first amended complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend to allege, if plaintiffs can do so in good faith, particular facts showing recklessness to support a claim for elder abuse. Plaintiffs' allegations of recklessness and understaffing in violation of regulations are conclusory. Paragraph 36 alleges that decedent was provided a sitter, but that the sitter monitored two patients rather than one and this violated the physician's order. This allegation pleads a negligent undertaking of medical services rather than recklessness or the refusal to provide basic needs. (Worsham v. O'Connor Hospital (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 331.) Paragraph 36 indicates that defendants implemented the care plan, but did so in a negligent manner. In Sababin v. Sup. Crt. (2006) 144 Cal.App.4th 81, 90, the evidence demonstrated more egregious conduct as the trier of fact could infer that the defendants ignored the care plan and failed to check the skin condition of the injured resident.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.
